rationale: Spot-checked the ~1400 new test lines by literal mutation-testing in a /tmp copy of the worktree (never touching the tracked worktree): (1) removed the substitutes nil->{} guard in gateResolvedJSON — TestConfigShowGate_ResolvedJSON correctly failed on the raw-string `"substitutes":null` check; (2) neutered escapeConfigValue to a no-op — TestConfigShow_HostileStringsEscaped failed and printed the actual forged `injected_key:` line, proving the assertion drives on raw stdout bytes, not a typed unmarshal; (3) reordered PanelGateKeys — TestConfigShow_GatesSubstitutesAndModelAdvisory's enum-order check failed; (4) dropped the adhoc->bead reviewer fallback — TestPanelGateResolvers_FallbackAndAdhoc failed; (5) added a wrong 'reset cursor on explicit lens' branch to expandSlots — TestPanelGateSlots_DeterministicExpansion failed with exactly the message the test's own comment predicts. Every load-bearing test I probed is falsifiable, not decorative. The 109 backward-compat test (byte-identical fixture, gates:{} equivalence, count-less->1 relaxation) and the panel.Gate decision-inertness test (all three gate values + legacy field-for-field parse) are equally concrete. The internal/complete nil-guard test's gateAwareAdvise helper reproduces complete.go's real call site (reg!=nil guard before PanelGateAdvisoryDefault) verbatim. Full suite passes; validateGates' error paths in config.go each map to a distinct case in TestLoad_RefusesPerGateKnobs's (a)-(h) table plus the unknown-model negative control.
findings: Verified via mutation (revert-and-observe) that 5 distinct load-bearing tests fail on the specific regression they claim to pin: substitutes-null, escaping/line-forging, gate-enum-order, adhoc-fallback resolver, and cursor-reset-on-explicit-lens.

TestConfigShow_HostileStringsEscaped and its cmd/mindspec sibling assert on raw stdout bytes (bytes.IndexByte / strings.HasPrefix scan for forged lines) before any JSON/struct decode, which is the correct falsifiability shape for an escaping test — a typed unmarshal would silently absorb a forging bug.

No decorative or vacuously-passing test found in the four diffed files; no obvious uncovered decision-surface edge case in the reviewed portion (validateGates' error returns each have a corresponding test case).

Minor (non-blocking) observation: TestPanelGateSlots_DeterministicExpansion's own comments accurately predict the exact wrong output a buggy cursor-reset variant would produce (author-of-record instead of empirical-prober) — confirmed byte-for-byte against my mutation, which is a good sign of intentional (not incidental) design.
